**Christophe Soumillon** (born 4 June 1981) is a Belgian [jockey](/wiki/Jockey "Jockey") based in France.

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Christophe_Soumillon&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

Coming from a [racing](/wiki/Horse_racing "Horse racing") background, Soumillon is the son of [jump](/wiki/Steeplechase_(horse_racing) "Steeplechase (horse racing)") jockey Jean-Marc Soumillon.[[1]](#cite_note-Sky-1) He rode in pony races and left Belgium aged fifteen to go to the racing school in [Chantilly](/wiki/Chantilly,_Oise "Chantilly, Oise"), [France](/wiki/France "France"), where he was then apprenticed to trainer Cédric Boutin.[[2]](#cite_note-II-2)

Soumillon rode his first [Group race](/wiki/Group_races "Group races") winner aged eighteen when Berine's Son, trained by [André Fabre](/wiki/Andr%C3%A9_Fabre "André Fabre"), won the 2000 [Prix de Fontainebleau](/wiki/Prix_de_Fontainebleau "Prix de Fontainebleau"). The following season he won four Group 1 races, including the [Prix du Jockey Club](/wiki/Prix_du_Jockey_Club "Prix du Jockey Club") on [Anabaa](/wiki/Anabaa "Anabaa").[[3]](#cite_note-Stats-3) In 2002 he was appointed first jockey in France to the [Aga Khan's racing operation](/wiki/Aga_Khan_IV#Thoroughbred_horse_racing "Aga Khan IV"), a position he held until 2009 and then again from 2014 to 2022.[[1]](#cite_note-Sky-1)[[4]](#cite_note-Post-4) The partnership was successful in the 2003 [Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe](/wiki/Prix_de_l%27Arc_de_Triomphe "Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe") with [Dalakhani](/wiki/Dalakhani "Dalakhani") and won the same race with the unbeaten filly [Zarkava](/wiki/Zarkava "Zarkava").[[4]](#cite_note-Post-4) In 2009 Soumillon lost his position, but the partnership resumed in 2014 and the following year they were successful at [Royal Ascot](/wiki/Royal_Ascot "Royal Ascot") when Ervedya won the [Coronation Stakes](/wiki/Coronation_Stakes "Coronation Stakes").[[4]](#cite_note-Post-4)

Soumillon won the cravache d'or (the golden whip or [French champion jockey](/wiki/French_flat_racing_Champion_Jockey "French flat racing Champion Jockey") title) for the first time in 2003 and won his tenth title in 2018.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Although primarily known as a flat jockey, Soumillon has also ridden over [hurdles](/wiki/Hurdling_(horse_race) "Hurdling (horse race)"), winning the 2010 [Grande Course de Haies d'Auteuil](/wiki/Grande_Course_de_Haies_d%27Auteuil "Grande Course de Haies d'Auteuil") (French Champion Hurdle) on Mandali.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

In March 2020, he surpassed Yves Saint-Martin by riding his 3,315th winner in France. This milestone cemented his status at the top of the all-time leaderboard for wins in France.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

On 30 September 2022 Soumillon was given a 60 day ban for elbowing [Rossa Ryan](/wiki/Rossa_Ryan "Rossa Ryan") off his horse during the [Prix Thomas Bryon](/wiki/Prix_Thomas_Bryon "Prix Thomas Bryon") at [Saint-Cloud](/wiki/Saint-Cloud_Racecourse "Saint-Cloud Racecourse").[[8]](#cite_note-8) He also lost his position as retained rider to the Aga Khan over the incident.[[9]](#cite_note-9) The Police des Jeux (the Gaming unit of the [Central Directorate of the Judicial Police](/wiki/Central_Directorate_of_the_Judicial_Police "Central Directorate of the Judicial Police")) considered extending Soumillon's ban but decided against it.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)

In July 2024, Soumillon won the [King George VI and Queen Elizabeth Stakes](/wiki/King_George_VI_and_Queen_Elizabeth_Stakes "King George VI and Queen Elizabeth Stakes") at [Ascot](/wiki/Ascot_Racecourse "Ascot Racecourse") on 25/1 chance Goliath, trained by Francis-Henry Gaffard. After the race he said that he dreamt of winning the [Epsom Derby](/wiki/Epsom_Derby "Epsom Derby") and invited trainers to offer him a ride in the race.[[12]](#cite_note-12) Trainer Charlie Johnstom offered him a ride in the [2025 Derby](/wiki/2025_Epsom_Derby "2025 Epsom Derby") on Lazy Griff, a syndicate-owned horse who started at odds of 50/1 and had been 100/1 at one time. Soumillon had already ridden him to win the Group 3 [Prix de Condé](/wiki/Prix_de_Cond%C3%A9 "Prix de Condé") at [Chantilly](/wiki/Chantilly_Racecourse "Chantilly Racecourse") the previous autumn. Lazy Griff defied his odds to achieve second place behind [Lambourn](/wiki/Lambourn_(horse) "Lambourn (horse)") in the Derby.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Christophe_Soumillon&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Soumillon married TV presenter and former [beauty queen](/wiki/Beauty_pageant "Beauty pageant") [Sophie Thalmann](/wiki/Sophie_Thalmann "Sophie Thalmann") on 22 July 2006. The couple have three children, daughter Charlie (born September 2005), son Mika (born November 2008) and son Robin (born April 2017).[[14]](#cite_note-14)

Having been involved with pony racing for several years, Soumillon founded the Soumillon International Pony Academy in [Deauville](/wiki/Deauville "Deauville") in 2023.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

## Major wins

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Christophe_Soumillon&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Major wins")]

[![France](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/c/c3/Flag_of_France.svg/40px-Flag_of_France.svg.png)](/wiki/France "France") **[France](/wiki/France "France")**

* [Critérium International](/wiki/Crit%C3%A9rium_International_(horse_race) "Critérium International (horse race)") - (5) - *[Dalakhani](/wiki/Dalakhani "Dalakhani") (2002), Carlotamix (2005), [Thunder Snow](/wiki/Thunder_Snow "Thunder Snow") (2016), Royal Meeting (2018), Puerto Rico (2025)*
* [Critérium de Saint-Cloud](/wiki/Crit%C3%A9rium_de_Saint-Cloud "Critérium de Saint-Cloud") - (4) - *Linda's Lad (2005), Prince Gibraltar (2013), Los Angeles (2023), Pierre Bonnard (2025)*
* [Grande Course de Haies d'Auteuil](/wiki/Grande_Course_de_Haies_d%27Auteuil "Grande Course de Haies d'Auteuil") - (1) - *Mandali (2010)*
* [Grand Prix de Paris](/wiki/Grand_Prix_de_Paris "Grand Prix de Paris") - (4) - *Khalkevi (2002), [Rail Link](/wiki/Rail_Link_(horse) "Rail Link (horse)") (2006), Montmartre (2008), Shakeel (2017)*
* [Grand Prix de Saint-Cloud](/wiki/Grand_Prix_de_Saint-Cloud "Grand Prix de Saint-Cloud") - (2) - *Mirio (2001), Zarak (2017)*
* [Poule d'Essai des Poulains](/wiki/Poule_d%27Essai_des_Poulains "Poule d'Essai des Poulains") - (3) - *Vahorimix (2001), Clodovil (2003), Silver Frost (2009)*
* [Poule d'Essai des Pouliches](/wiki/Poule_d%27Essai_des_Pouliches "Poule d'Essai des Pouliches") - (5) - *Musical Chimes (2003), Darjina (2007), [Zarkava](/wiki/Zarkava "Zarkava") (2008), Beauty Parlour (2012), Ervedya (2015)*
